notes: REAL SAFETY-RELEVANT FINDING: on the recall-kickoff eval, the with-skill run checked live PLM/Complaint Log data and found that 'lot 24-11' doesn't match any real Soursop Vit C batch record — it correctly stopped at Gate 1 rather than scoping a recall against an unverified batch number. Baseline never checked and accepted the batch number at face value. This is exactly the kind of caution that matters on a recall workflow.

Baseline never made a single live Asana/PLM/wiki tool call across any of the three evals (3 tool calls flat every time, likely just file reads) — it reasoned entirely from general knowledge and the prompt text. That's consistent with good general judgment (e.g. correctly citing the real MoCRA 15-business-day FDA clock on the SAE eval) but a real blind spot for a skill whose job is intake against live customer/batch records.

With-skill pass rate 100% across all three evals; baseline 17-33%, lowest on the SAE eval specifically — the one eval where baseline substituted its own generic triage framework instead of referencing the actual internal SOP (SKN-OPS-002) at all.
